title: npm Scope Naming — One Scope per Audience
summary: Prior-art survey grounding backlog #2155: how comparable ecosystems map npm scopes to audiences and products before the constellation's first Plateau/FUI publish. Surveyed splits (@types, @angular vs @angular-devkit, @vue vs @vitejs, @lit vs @lit-labs, @web, @open-wc, @astrojs, the Shoelace → Web Awesome forced migration) show a scope earns existence via a distinct public product identity or a support-policy channel — never via privacy, since npm access is per-package, not per-scope. npm mechanics bound the call: a scope is an org (near-impossible to rename — republish-everything is the official path), restricted → public is a one-flag flip while public is effectively irreversible, and provenance requires public. Feeds three prepared forks: the canonical FUI scope spelling (@frontierui, matching all 14 in-tree package names), the Plateau scope count (one live customer scope, @plateaudev/@plateauapp as defensive holds with a named re-open trigger), and the access posture (restricted-until-an-explicit-go for impl/product scopes; public for the @webeverything standard surface per #907).
